task_completion.description: Create a new C# Shared Project named 'SecurityCore'
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Solution Explorer in Visual Studio shows a project named 'SecurityCore' (typically with a Shared Project icon).

task_completion.description: Extract 'CryptoEngine.cs' into the new Shared Project
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Solution Explorer shows the file 'CryptoEngine.cs' located inside the 'SecurityCore' project.

task_completion.description: Remove the duplicate 'CryptoEngine.cs' files from the original projects
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Solution Explorer does NOT show 'CryptoEngine.cs' directly under the 'IncidentAgent' or 'AuditTool' project file trees.

task_completion.description: Reference the 'SecurityCore' project from both 'IncidentAgent' and 'AuditTool'
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Solution Explorer shows 'SecurityCore' listed under the 'Dependencies' -> 'Projects' (or 'Shared Projects') node for both the 'IncidentAgent' and 'AuditTool' projects.

task_completion.description: Define the conditional compilation symbol 'IR_AGENT' for the 'IncidentAgent' project
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Visual Studio Project Properties window for 'IncidentAgent' (Build -> General) shows 'IR_AGENT' entered in the 'Conditional compilation symbols' text box.

task_completion.description: Define the conditional compilation symbol 'AUDIT_TOOL' for the 'AuditTool' project
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Visual Studio Project Properties window for 'AuditTool' (Build -> General) shows 'AUDIT_TOOL' entered in the 'Conditional compilation symbols' text box.

task_completion.description: The final solution must rebuild without errors
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Visual Studio Output window or Error List shows a successful build message (e.g., 'Rebuild All: 3 succeeded, 0 failed' or '0 errors') after the changes.

integrity.description: The agent used the Visual Studio 2022 GUI to perform the refactoring and configuration
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interactions within the Visual Studio IDE (Solution Explorer, Properties windows, Output panel) rather than just editing .csproj files in a basic text editor or using terminal commands.

integrity.description: The build success message comes from the actual Visual Studio build process
integrity.visual_evidence: The build output is visible in the integrated Visual Studio Output window or Error List, showing standard MSBuild logs, not a mocked text file or manually typed terminal output.

integrity.description: The agent is working within the correct 'SecurityToolsSuite.sln' solution
integrity.visual_evidence: The top of the Solution Explorer clearly displays 'Solution SecurityToolsSuite' containing the required projects.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The shared project must be named exactly 'SecurityCore' (standard extension .shproj). The conditional compilation symbol for the IncidentAgent project must be exactly 'IR_AGENT'. The conditional compilation symbol for the AuditTool project must be exactly 'AUDIT_TOOL'.
